name: East Dean
overview: East Dean is a small civil parish and village in the Test Valley district of Hampshire, lying on the south bank of the River Dun about 6 miles north-west of Romsey and close to the Wiltshire border. Recorded in the Domesday Book as Dene, it sits in the wooded Dun valley on the Salisbury to Southampton Wessex Main Line and has its own parish church. The neighbouring villages of West Tytherley and Lockerley provide most day-to-day services.
character: A quiet, scattered rural settlement of farmland and woodland in the Dun valley, with a strong commuter-belt feel given its mainline railway access toward Salisbury and Southampton.
amenities: Limited services in the village itself
Post office and village store in nearby West Tytherley and Lockerley
Village halls, recreation ground and play area in neighbouring West Tytherley

commutes.notes: From Dean station on the South Western Railway Salisbury to Southampton line (unstaffed halt)
About 6 miles south-east via the A27
Direct from Dean station toward Southampton Central

prices.summary: Predominantly detached and period village houses; sold prices in the SP5 area typically range from around 400,000 to over 800,000 pounds depending on size and plot.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling in the Dun valley and surrounding woodland
Country pub dining at the Black Horse in nearby West Tytherley
Easy rail day trips to Salisbury and the New Forest
placesToEat: The Black Horse, West Tytherley (17th-century Grade II coaching inn with real ales and food)
pros: Mainline rail access to Salisbury and Southampton from Dean station
Quiet rural setting close to the Wiltshire border
Good local primary schools within a few miles
cons: Very few amenities in the village itself
Reliance on a car or train for shops and services
Dean station is an unstaffed halt with a limited service
whoItSuits: Rail commuters and families wanting a quiet Test Valley village within easy reach of Salisbury and Southampton.

sources.label: West Tytherley CofE Primary School - GOV.UK
